explanation: The Sigma rule detects processes like WerFault.exe when they are executed without arguments. This is a trivial example of a sacrificial process with improper arguments. The command 'WerFault.exe' matches the rule because its command line ends with the process name without additional parameters.

The Sigma rule detects rundll32.exe when executed without arguments. This is a common bad OpSec default in tooling like Cobalt Strike. The command 'rundll32.exe' matches the rule because its command line ends with the process name without additional parameters.

The Sigma rule detects regsvr32.exe when executed without arguments. This is a trivial example of a sacrificial process with improper arguments. The command 'regsvr32.exe' matches the rule because its command line ends with the process name without additional parameters.

The Sigma rule detects regasm.exe when executed without arguments. This is a trivial example of a sacrificial process with improper arguments. The command 'regasm.exe' matches the rule because its command line ends with the process name without additional parameters.

The Sigma rule detects regsvcs.exe when executed without arguments. This is a trivial example of a sacrificial process with improper arguments. The command 'regsvcs.exe' matches the rule because its command line ends with the process name without additional parameters.
